stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (solar panels) yields a guaranteed return of $184K. Option B (wind turbines) depends on weather patterns: with probability 40-64% (uncertain), it yields $426K; otherwise it yields $86K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in weather patterns affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $184K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $222.0K to $303.6K. Point estimate: $262.8K. Option B is preferred at most assumptions (EV_B > EV_A when P(good) > 28.8%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 28.8%, which falls outside the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
